title: Rinorea calycina
intro: Rinorea calycina, also known as the African medinilla, is a plant species in the family Violaceae, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea calycina include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Rinorea calycina is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared for use in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Rinorea calycina belongs to the family Violaceae. It is a species of flowering plant within the genus Rinorea. The genus Rinorea is a part of the larger clade known as Ericales in the order Malpighiales. Rinorea calycina is native to tropical Africa. Its classification is closely related to other flowering plants in the Malpighiales order.
